name: flask-flatpages

metadata.name: Flask-FlatPages
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Provides flat static pages to a Flask application

documentation:
# Flask-FlatPages

```{image} https://github.com/flask-FlatPages/flask-FlatPages/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://github.com/flask-FlatPages/flask-FlatPages/actions/workflows/test.yml/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/Flask-FlatPages.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Flask-FlatPages
```

Provides flat static pages to a [Flask] application, based on text files
as opposed to a relational database.

- Works on Python 2.7 and 3.6+
- BSD licensed
- Latest documentation [on Read the Docs]
- Source, issues and pull requests [on Github]
- Releases [on PyPI]
- Install with `pip install Flask-FlatPages`

[flask]: http://flask.pocoo.org/
[on github]: https://github.com/SimonSapin/Flask-FlatPages/
[on pypi]: http://pypi.python.org/pypi/Flask-FlatPages
[on read the docs]: http://flask-flatpages.readthedocs.org/

modules.name: flask_flatpages
documentation:
Flask-FlatPages provides a collection of pages to your Flask application.

Pages are built from "flat" text files as opposed to a relational database.

:copyright: (c) 2010-2015 by Simon Sapin, 2013-2015 by Igor Davydenko.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

modules.name: flask_flatpages.imports
documentation: Conditional imports.

modules.name: flask_flatpages.utils
documentation: Utility functions to render Markdown text to HTML.

functions.name: flask_flatpages.utils.force_unicode
documentation: Convert bytes or any other Python instance to string.

functions.name: flask_flatpages.utils.pygmented_markdown
documentation:
Render Markdown text to HTML.

Uses the [CodeHilite] extension only if [Pygments] is available. If
[Pygments] is not available, "codehilite" is removed from list of
extensions.

If you need other extensions, set them up using the
`FLATPAGES_MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` setting, which should be a sequence
of strings or Markdown Extension objects.
Extensions specified with entrypoint strings should be configured using
`FLATPAGES_EXTENSION_CONFIGS`.

[codehilite]: http://www.freewisdom.org/projects/python-markdown/CodeHilite
[pygments]: http://pygments.org/

functions.name: flask_flatpages.utils.pygments_style_defs
documentation:
---
param style: The Pygments [style] to use.
return: the CSS definitions for the [CodeHilite] Markdown plugin.
---

Only available if [Pygments] is.

[codehilite]: http://www.freewisdom.org/projects/python-markdown/CodeHilite
[pygments]: http://pygments.org/
[style]: http://pygments.org/docs/styles/

documentation:
Initialise the NamedStringIO.

```
param content

The string to be treated as a stream

param name

The name to attach to the stream. Will
be consumed in e.g. ReaderErrors raised by pyyaml.
```
Subclass adding a Name to {class}`StringIO` objects.

modules.name: flask_flatpages.page
documentation: Define flatpage instance.

classes.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Page instance.

```
param path

Page path.

param meta

Page meta data in YAML format.

param body

Page body.

param html_renderer

HTML renderer function.

param folder

The folder the page is contained in.
```

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.__getitem__
documentation:
Shortcut for accessing metadata.

`page['title']` or, in a template, `{{ page.title }}` are
equivalent to `page.meta['title']`.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.__html__
documentation:
Return HTML for use in Jinja templates.

In a template, `{{ page }}` is equivalent to
`{{ page.html|safe }}`.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.__repr__
documentation: Machine representation of {class}`Page` instance.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.html
documentation: Content of the page, rendered as HTML by the configured renderer.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.page.Page.meta
documentation: Store a dict of metadata parsed from the YAML header of the file.

Simple class to store all necessary information about a flatpage.

Main purpose is to render the page's content with a `html_renderer`
function.

modules.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages
documentation: Flatpages extension.

classes.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.__init__
documentation:
Initialize FlatPages extension.

```
param app

Your application. Can be omitted if you call
 later.

type app

A  instance

param name

The name for this FlatPages instance. Used for looking
up config values using

source:8: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

'FLATPAGES_%s_%s' % (name.upper(), key)

source:9: (WARNING/2) Block quote ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

By default, no name is used, so configuration is
done by specifying config values using

source:11: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

'FLATPAGES_%s' % (key)

source:12: (WARNING/2) Block quote ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

Typically, you only need to set this parameter if you
want to use multiple :class:`FlatPages instances within the
same Flask application.

source:12: (WARNING/2) Inline interpreted text or phrase reference start-string without end-string.

type name

string
```

:::{versionchanged} 0.6
New parameter `name` to support multiple FlatPages instances.
:::

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.__iter__
documentation: Iterate on all {class}`Page` objects.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.config
documentation:
Read actual configuration from Flask application config.

```
param key

Lowercase config key from  tuple
```

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.get
documentation:
Return the {class}`Page` object at `path`.

Returns `default` if there is no such page.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.get_or_404
documentation:
Return the {class}`Page` object at `path`.

Raise Flask's 404 error if there is no such page.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.init_app
documentation:
Use to initialize an application.

Ueful for passing an app later and app factory patterns.

```
param app

your application

type app

a  instance
```

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.app
documentation:
The Flask Application associated with this extension.

Accessing the application instance this way is not advised. This
property exists to ensure backwards compatability, and in future
will wrap `flask.current_app`, the recommended way to access
the app instance.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.reload
documentation:
Forget all pages.

All pages will be reloaded next time they're accessed.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages.root
documentation:
Full path to the directory where pages are looked for.

This corresponds to the `FLATPAGES_%(name)s_ROOT` config value,
interpreted as relative to the app's root directory, or as relative
to the app's instance folder if `FLATPAGES_%(name)s_INSTANCE_RELATIVE`
is set to `True`.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages._conditional_auto_reset
documentation: Reset if configured to do so on new requests.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages._load_file
documentation:
Load file from file system and cache it.

We store the result as a tuple of {class}`Path` and the file `mtime`.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages._pages
documentation:
Walk the page root directory and return a dict of pages.

Returns a dictionary of pages keyed by their path.

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages._parse
documentation:
Parse a flatpage file, i.e. read and parse its meta data and body.

```
return

initialized  instance.
```

methods.name: flask_flatpages.flatpages.FlatPages._smart_html_renderer
documentation:
Wrappper to enable  rendering functions with differing signatures.

We stay backwards compatible by using reflection, i.e. we inspect the
given rendering function's signature in order to find out how many
arguments the function takes.

:::{versionchanged} 0.6
Support for HTML renderer functions with signature
`f(body, flatpages, page)`, where `page` is an instance of
{class}`Page`.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.5
Support for HTML renderer functions with signature
`f(body, flatpages)`, where `flatpages` is an instance of
{class}`FlatPages`.
:::
A collection of {class}`Page` objects.
